clues.clue: This New York baseball club, the 'Bronx Bombers,' has won 27 World Series.
This Green Bay NFL team takes its name from a meatpacking sponsor.
This Boston NBA team sports a leprechaun logo and 17 banners.
Fans of this 'Steel City' NFL team wave Terrible Towels.
This Pittsburgh NHL club, 'the Pens,' has won multiple Stanley Cups.
Basketball's 'His Airness' wore number 23 for the Chicago Bulls.
This boxer called himself 'The Greatest' and floated like a butterfly.
Hockey's 'Great One' wore number 99 and shattered scoring records.
This Argentine soccer legend, nicknamed 'D10S,' won the 1986 World Cup.
Baseball's 'Sultan of Swat' clouted 714 career home runs.
This fuzzy green Philadelphia baseball mascot has a long snout and a cannon.
The San Diego Padres' mascot is a brown-robed, smiling religious brother.
The University of Texas parades a live longhorn steer by this name.
The Phoenix Suns' costumed mascot is a purple, dunking ape.
Notre Dame's pugnacious 'Fighting Irish' mascot is this little fellow with raised fists.
This Boston ballpark, opened in 1912, has a tall left-field wall called the 'Green Monster.'
The Yankees–Red Sox feud pits New York against this New England city.
College football's 'Iron Bowl' pits Auburn against this in-state Crimson Tide rival.
This ivy-walled Chicago ballpark is home to the Cubs.
The storied Lakers–Celtics rivalry pits these two basketball cities against each other.
clues.response: What are Los Angeles and Boston?

clues.clue: The Chicago Bulls' primary uniform color is this fiery hue.
The Dallas Cowboys' helmets feature this five-pointed shape.
The Pittsburgh Steelers pair black with this shining color.
The Green Bay Packers' two colors are green and this.
The Raiders' menacing look pairs a metallic gray hue with the color of night.

final.clue: These five interlocking colored loops symbolize the union of the world's continents at the Games.
